concepts.label: AI agents
concepts.def_snippet: AI agents are software systems that can perceive their environment, make decisions, and take actions toward goals with minimal human intervention.

concepts.label: agentic workflows
concepts.def_snippet: Agentic workflows are systems where AI agents autonomously execute multi-step tasks by perceiving conditions, making decisions, and taking actions toward defined goals.

concepts.label: automation
concepts.def_snippet: Automation uses software or AI systems to perform repetitive tasks or workflows without human intervention, increasing efficiency and reducing manual effort.

concepts.label: test-time compute

concepts.label: prompt engineering
concepts.def_snippet: Prompt engineering is the practice of crafting and refining input text to elicit desired outputs from language models.

concepts.label: agentic AI
concepts.def_snippet: Agentic AI refers to AI systems that autonomously perceive their environment, make decisions, and take actions toward goals rather than simply responding to prompts.

concepts.label: code generation
concepts.def_snippet: Code generation is a tool that automatically writes or completes computer code based on natural language descriptions, code context, or examples provided by users.

concepts.label: multi-agent systems

concepts.label: workflow orchestration
concepts.def_snippet: Workflow orchestration is a tool that automates the coordination and sequencing of multiple tasks or AI agents to complete complex processes end-to-end.

concepts.label: video generation
concepts.def_snippet: A video generation tool synthesizes realistic video sequences from text prompts, images, or other inputs by learning temporal and spatial patterns from training data.

concepts.label: large language models
concepts.def_snippet: Large language models are neural networks trained on vast text corpora to predict and generate human language by processing tokens sequentially through transformer architectures.

concepts.label: benchmark
concepts.def_snippet: A benchmark is a standardized test or dataset that measures and compares the performance of AI systems on specific tasks or capabilities.

concepts.label: AI-assisted coding
concepts.def_snippet: AI-assisted coding is a technique where language models suggest or generate code snippets, functions, or entire programs based on developer input, reducing manual writing and accel…

concepts.label: generative AI
concepts.def_snippet: Generative AI refers to systems trained to produce new content such as text, images, or code by learning patterns from existing data.

concepts.label: skills
concepts.def_snippet: A skill is a discrete, composable capability that an AI agent can learn or be given to perform specific tasks, from code execution to tool use.

concepts.label: data integration
concepts.def_snippet: Data integration combines information from multiple sources into a unified format or system, enabling models to access diverse data for training or inference.

concepts.label: AGI

concepts.label: market dynamics
concepts.def_snippet: Market dynamics in AI discourse refers to questions about how competitive positioning, funding, valuation, and business model viability will reshape the AI industry landscape.

concepts.label: LLMs
concepts.def_snippet: Large language models are AI systems trained on vast text data to predict and generate human language, raising questions about their capabilities, limitations, and societal impact.

concepts.label: computer use
concepts.def_snippet: Computer use is an AI capability that enables models to control computers by viewing screens and operating keyboards and mice to accomplish tasks autonomously.

concepts.label: agent design
concepts.def_snippet: An AI agent design is a system architecture that equips language models with tools, memory, and decision-making loops to autonomously accomplish multi-step tasks.

concepts.label: autoresearch
concepts.def_snippet: Autoresearch is a tool that automatically identifies knowledge gaps in Claude's responses and conducts research to fill them without manual intervention.

concepts.label: LLM
concepts.def_snippet: A large language model is a neural network trained on vast text data to predict and generate human language, forming the foundation for most current AI assistants.

concepts.label: recursive self-improvement

concepts.label: task parallelization
concepts.def_snippet: Task parallelization divides a job into independent subtasks that execute simultaneously across multiple processors or threads to reduce overall completion time.

concepts.label: agent wrapper
concepts.def_snippet: An agent wrapper is a software framework that manages the interface between an AI agent and external tools or systems, handling input/output formatting and execution logic.

concepts.label: vision-language models
concepts.def_snippet: Vision-language models are neural networks trained on paired image and text data to perform tasks requiring understanding of both visual content and natural language descriptions.

concepts.label: orchestration
concepts.def_snippet: Orchestration is a system or framework that coordinates multiple AI agents, models, or processes to work together sequentially or in parallel toward a shared goal.

concepts.label: personalization
concepts.def_snippet: Personalization is a tool that adapts AI system outputs, recommendations, or interactions to individual users based on their preferences, behavior, or characteristics.

concepts.label: scheduling
concepts.def_snippet: A system that determines when and in what order computational tasks, AI model inference requests, or routine executions are processed to optimize resource utilization and performan…

concepts.label: fine-tuning
concepts.def_snippet: Fine-tuning is the process of training a pre-trained model on task-specific data to adapt its weights for particular applications or domains.

concepts.label: YAML workflows
concepts.def_snippet: YAML workflows are configuration files that define multi-step processes for AI agents, specifying task sequences, conditions, and dependencies in a human-readable format.

concepts.label: memory systems

concepts.label: context stacking
concepts.def_snippet: Context stacking is a technique that organizes and layers multiple pieces of information or documents to help language models process complex tasks by maintaining relevant informat…

concepts.label: AI agency strategy
concepts.def_snippet: AI agency strategy refers to business approaches for building and scaling consulting or services firms that implement AI solutions for clients.

concepts.label: AI regulation
concepts.def_snippet: AI regulation refers to government rules and legal frameworks that govern the development, deployment, and use of artificial intelligence systems to address safety, privacy, bias,…

concepts.label: evaluation
concepts.def_snippet: Evaluation is a systematic process that measures how well an AI model or system performs on specific tasks or datasets to assess its capabilities and limitations.

concepts.label: prompt chaining
concepts.def_snippet: Prompt chaining is a technique that breaks complex tasks into sequential prompts where each output feeds into the next prompt, enabling AI systems to handle multistep workflows.

concepts.label: local inference
concepts.def_snippet: Local inference is running AI models on a user's own device or server rather than sending data to remote cloud services.

concepts.label: cross-attention

concepts.label: software engineering
concepts.def_snippet: Software engineering is the discipline of designing, building, testing, and maintaining software systems using structured methodologies and best practices.

concepts.label: reinforcement learning
concepts.def_snippet: Reinforcement learning is a machine learning technique where an agent learns to make sequential decisions by receiving rewards or penalties for its actions in an environment.

concepts.label: cybersecurity
concepts.def_snippet: Cybersecurity is the practice of protecting computer systems, networks, and data from unauthorized access, theft, and damage.

concepts.label: deep learning
concepts.def_snippet: Deep learning is a machine learning technique using artificial neural networks with multiple layers to learn hierarchical patterns in data for tasks like classification, prediction…

concepts.label: model-agnostic
concepts.def_snippet: A model-agnostic tool works with any underlying AI model, enabling flexibility to swap models without redesigning the system.

concepts.label: compute efficiency
concepts.def_snippet: The question of whether AI progress should prioritize maximizing capabilities or minimizing the computational resources required to achieve them.

concepts.label: multi-model workflow
concepts.def_snippet: Multi-model workflow is a technique that routes tasks to different AI models based on their strengths, combining complementary capabilities to improve overall performance on comple…

concepts.label: world models
concepts.def_snippet: World models are learned representations of environmental dynamics that enable agents to predict future states and plan actions without real-world interaction.

concepts.label: multi-platform integration
concepts.def_snippet: A tool that connects multiple software platforms to enable workflows where actions in one application automatically trigger corresponding actions in another.

concepts.label: async agents
concepts.def_snippet: Async agents are AI systems that execute tasks independently over time without requiring continuous user interaction, useful for automating multi-step workflows that don't need rea…

concepts.label: adversarial AI
concepts.def_snippet: Adversarial AI refers to AI systems designed or manipulated to deceive, attack, or circumvent other AI systems or human oversight through intentionally deceptive behavior.

concepts.label: agent optimization

concepts.label: AI risk
concepts.def_snippet: AI risk refers to the concern that advanced AI systems could cause harm through unintended consequences, misuse, or loss of human control.
